mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed is in a constrained easing regime. This signals future monetary tightening is imminent, causing equity risk premiums to widen and S&P 500 forward returns to decline over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-11-01, FEDFUNDS=0.08% and CPIAUCSL=278.88, indicating near-zero rates with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-2021.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over a 3-month rolling window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 2021-11-01 was 149,230 thousand, near post-pandemic recovery peak
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following PAYEMS declines are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10), or mechanism fails to trigger in any 12-month period where unemployment rises > 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust consumer demand and supports equity valuations. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 2021-11-01 was 624,874 million dollars, reflecting strong post-pandemic consumer activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following strong RSAFS months are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while remaining positive, signaling a late-cycle slowdown in manufacturing capacity utilization. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases and profit margins compress.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 2021-10-01 was 100.19 (revised), indicating near-trend production levels
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of INDPRO deceleration (0-0.2% growth) are positive in > 60% of instances, or mechanism produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,500 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2% annualized, signaling robust demand-side momentum. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as construction and related equity sectors benefit.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2021-10-01 was 1,579 thousand units; GDPC1 at 2021-07-01 was 21,617.828 billion (revised)
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month, signaling a labor market inflection point. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as equity markets reprrice recession risk rapidly.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 2021-11-01 was 4.1%; PAYEMS was 149,230 thousand
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or mechanism fails to trigger in any 6-month period where unemployment rises > 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 1.5% in a tightening cycle and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 270 index points, real rates turn restrictive. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress under higher discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-11-01, FEDFUNDS=0.08% and CPIAUCSL=278.88, indicating this regime was not yet active
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) declines, signaling strong supply-side productivity gains and tight labor markets. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand and wage pressures remain contained.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.5% AND UNRATE monthly change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 2021-10-01 was 100.19; UNRATE at 2021-11-01 was 4.1%
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
